function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the public internet for information. Each result will contain a url, a title, and one excerpt taken directly from the page.

start_date.description: (optional) The start date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Must not be equal to end_date.

end_date.description: (optional) The end date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. If the value is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

number_of_results.description: (optional) The number of search results to return.

function.name: retrieve_information
function.description: Retrieves critical data segments from a previously ingested document via hybrid lexical and semantic calculations. Crucial Requirement: The search prompt argument MUST contain the token placeholder containing your storage key inside double curly brackets, exactly format formatted as: {{key_name}} alongside your query description text.

prompt.description: Your target question containing the explicit target reference mapping (e.g., 'What were the total research costs in {{my_document_key}}?').

preserve_document_order.description: Set to True if processing complex structural data dependencies across sequential disclosures.

function.name: parse_html_page
function.description: Parses an HTML document or SEC filing. It automatically extracts key metadata, converts asset tables to clean markdown, slices sections into optimal word chunks, and runs concurrent contextual LLM enrichment before updating the disk cache.

url.description: The target URL of the HTML page/filing to process.

key.description: The unique cache storage key for saving and loading the index.

function.name: edgar_search
function.description: Search the EDGAR Database through the SEC API. You should provide a search query. You can also optionally provide a start date, an end date, a page number, top N results, a list of form types, and/or a list of CIKs. The results are returned as a list of dictionaries, each containing the metadata for a filing. It does not contain the full text of the filing.

search_query.description: The case-insensitive search-term or phrase to search the contents of filings and their attachments. This can be a single word, phrase, or combination of words and phrases. Supported search features include wildcards (*), Boolean operators (OR, NOT), and exact phrase matching by enclosing phrases in quotation marks ("exact phrase"). By default, all terms are joined by an implicit AND operator.

form_types.description: (optional) Limits search to specific EDGAR form types (e.g., ['8-K', '10-Q']) list of strings. Default: all form types

ciks.description: (optional) Filters results to filings from specified CIKs, type list of strings. Leading zeros are optional but may be included. Example: [ "0001811414", "1318605" ]. Default: all CIKs

start_date.description: (optional) Start date for the search range in yyyy-mm-dd format. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

end_date.description: (optional) End date for the search range, in the same format as startDate. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

page.description: (optional) Used for pagination. Each page contains up to 100 matching filings. Increase the page number to retrieve the next set of 100 filings. Example: 3 retrieves the third page. Default: 1

top_n_results.description: (optional) Return only the first N results out of 100 from the page. If not provided, all 100 results will be returned. E.g. if page is 2, and top_n_results is 10, you will receive results 100 to 110.

function.name: calculator
function.description: Evaluate a mathematical expression and return the result. Use this tool for all arithmetic calculations instead of computing by hand. Supports: +, -, *, /, ** (exponentiation), % (modulo), and parentheses for grouping. Available functions: abs(), min(), max(), sqrt(), log(), log10(). Examples: '(5000 - 3200) * 0.21', '(2865507 / 1905871) ** 0.5 - 1', '14060 / 2148'.

function.name: price_history
function.description: Fetch historical daily price data for a specific asset class. Returns a CSV table with one row per day. Use asset_class='equity' or 'etf' for US-listed stocks/ETFs (e.g. AAPL, SPY), 'crypto' for pairs like btcusd (lowercase, no dash), or 'fx' for pairs like audusd. Non-US equities and most indices/futures are not covered by the pricing provider. Each row includes raw OHLC (open/high/low/close), split- and dividend-adjusted OHLC (adjOpen/adjHigh/adjLow/adjClose), raw and adjusted volume, divCash (dividend amount on the date), and splitFactor.

ticker.description: Ticker symbol. High-level symbology rules: (1) Equity/ETF/mutual fund: use the US exchange-listed symbol (AAPL, SPY, VFIAX). Symbols are case-insensitive and contain only letters and dashes. (2) Share classes use a dash, not a dot: 'BRK-A' / 'BRK-B' (not 'BRK.A'), 'BF-B'. (3) Securities are identified by the ticker as listed on the primary US exchange; non-US listings, OTC pink sheets, indices, and futures are generally not covered. (4) Tickers can be reused after delisting; data is point-in-time for the entity that held the ticker on each date. (5) Crypto: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'btcusd', 'ethusd'. (6) FX: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'audusd', 'eurusd'.

asset_class.description: Asset class used to route to the proper pricing endpoint.

function.name: submit_final_result
function.description: Submits the final answer to the user. You should include your final answer, as well as any necessary reasoning, justification, calculations, and explanation. Finally, you should provide any sources used to answer the question. You MUST use this tool to submit your final result. The user will not see your response if you do not use this tool to submit. You will not be able to continue working after this tool is called; the conversation will be ended.

final_result.description: The final result to submit to the user

steps.message:
You are an IPO and financial diligence agent.

You are given a question and must answer it using the tools provided.

You cannot interact with the user or ask clarifying questions.

Answer solely based on available information and tools.

Assume the current date is May 31, 2026.

---

TOOLS AND DATA SOURCES

You have access to a document storage system.

You may store parsed HTML retrieved from the web and later retrieve
information using LLM-based retrieval prompts.

Use this system to avoid context-window limitations.

When working with long filings, prefer section-specific retrieval prompts
rather than broad document-level retrieval.

Examples:

- retrieve the Segment Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation table from MD&A
- retrieve the glossary definition of Starshield
- retrieve the controlled-company governance disclosure

For peer-comparison questions:

- retrieve peer SEC filings using edgar_search
- do not rely on memory for peer financial figures

For historical public-equity price data not available in SEC filings:

- use price_history as primary source
- use raw unadjusted prices unless adjusted prices are requested
- fall back to web_search only if price_history fails

Stock indices are not covered by price_history.

Use authoritative sources such as FRED or web_search for index levels.

---

SOURCE HIERARCHY

Prioritize sources in this order:

1. S-1 / S-1A filings
2. Incorporated SEC filings
3. SEC exhibits and merger documents
4. SEC periodic filings
5. Company earnings materials
6. External sources

If figures differ across sources:

prefer the most authoritative SEC source containing the directly reported
figure and explain material discrepancies when relevant.

SEC filings are the primary and most authoritative source of financial and
disclosure information.

---

ANSWER STANDARDS

You must call submit_final_result with the completed answer.

Your submission will not be processed unless submit_final_result is called.

Include:

- complete reasoning
- calculations
- supporting analysis

You will be evaluated on:

- final-answer accuracy
- analytical correctness

Do not round intermediate calculations.

Round only final reported values.

When possible, report calculated figures to at least two decimal places.

Report financial figures using the same scale and units used in the filing
unless otherwise specified.

Share prices should be reported with two decimal places.

---

DISCLOSURE DISCIPLINE

Before reaching a conclusion:

determine whether the filing provides sufficient information to answer:

- directly
- partially
- only through inference

Distinguish carefully among:

- facts explicitly disclosed
- reasonable filing-supported inferences
- information not disclosed

Do not speculate beyond what the filing supports.

If material information is absent:

explicitly state that the filing does not provide sufficient information to
conclude.

Absence of disclosure may itself be analytically material.

---

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

Treat separately:

- historical facts
- roadmap targets
- commercialization expectations
- management intentions
- forward-looking statements

Do not present aspirations or plans as historical outcomes.

---

GOVERNANCE AND LEGAL QUESTIONS

For governance, legal, or disclosure-language questions:

use the filing's precise terminology whenever possible.

Distinguish among:

- requirements
- exemptions
- company elections
- forward-looking language

---

ACCOUNTING POLICY QUESTIONS

For accounting-policy questions:

1. Identify the relevant disclosed accounting policy or treatment
2. Determine whether the filing explicitly resolves the issue
3. If unresolved, state that the filing does not specify treatment

---

NON-GAAP QUESTIONS

For non-GAAP measures:

identify:

- the starting GAAP metric
- each disclosed adjustment
- whether economically recurring costs are excluded

Do not accept non-GAAP measures without analyzing their construction.

---

AUDIENCE TAGS

Questions may include audience tags such as:

[PUBLIC | BANK]
[VC | CREDIT]
[LEGAL | ACCOUNTING]

Use these tags to calibrate terminology and analytical emphasis while
remaining grounded in disclosed facts.

---

SOURCES

At the end of your answer provide:

{
  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "...",
      "name": "..."
    }
  ]
}


---


FILING CONTEXT

The primary filing for this benchmark is the SpaceX S-1 filed May 20, 2026:

https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm

Important:

The consolidated financial statements have been retrospectively recast under
common-control accounting to include:

- xAI (merged February 2, 2026)
- X Holdings (merged into xAI March 28, 2025)

for all historical periods presented.

No period reflects standalone SpaceX operations exclusively.

When interpreting historical segment figures, state whether they reflect the
recast consolidated entity.

SpaceX (SPCX) has no public trading history as of May 30, 2026.

Do not attempt price_history retrieval for SpaceX or SPCX.

For peer valuation or peer financial benchmarking, retrieve peer-company SEC
filings using edgar_search rather than relying on memory.

Question:
Common-control recast accounting presents historical periods as if entities had always been combined. What investor-interpretability or transparency concerns arise when historical standalone performance becomes less observable?

steps.reasoning_content:
The user is asking about the interpretability and transparency concerns that arise from common-control recast accounting, specifically in the context of the SpaceX S-1 filing which retrospectively recast financial statements to include xAI and X Holdings. This is an analytical/qualitative question that I need to ground in the actual filing disclosure.

Let me first retrieve the relevant disclosure from the SpaceX S-1 about common-control accounting and then provide a thorough analytical answer.

results.content: SUCCESS: Loaded portable assets for key: spacex_s1

steps.reasoning_content: Now let me retrieve the specific disclosures about common-control accounting and the recast methodology.

arguments.prompt: Retrieve all disclosures about common-control accounting, recast financial statements, retrospective combination of xAI and X Holdings, and any notes about historical periods being presented as if entities had always been combined from {{spacex_s1}}. Include the basis of presentation and any related accounting policy discussions.

results.content:
Based on the provided prospectus excerpts, here are the key disclosures regarding the common-control accounting, retrospective financial statements, and the combination of xAI and X Holdings.

### **1. Common-Control Accounting**
The mergers between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings were accounted for as reorganizations of entities under common control.
*   **Reason:** Elon Musk had a controlling financial interest (majority voting interest) in SpaceX, xAI, and X throughout all periods presented in the consolidated financial statements.
*   **Accounting Treatment:** This requires the consolidated financial statements to be prepared as if the combining entities had always been combined. The transactions are treated as a continuity of interests, not as an acquisition by a third party.

### **2. Retrospective Combination & Recast Financial Statements**
The historical financial statements have been retroactively recast to present the combined entity as if it existed for all periods.
*   **Net Assets:** Presented at their historical carrying amounts for all periods. No new goodwill or other intangible assets were recorded as a result of the mergers.
*   **Elimination of Transactions:** All historical related-party transactions between the entities (SpaceX, xAI, and X) have been eliminated in consolidation.
*   **Equity Presentation:** Capital stock and shareholders' equity reflect a continuation of the historical SpaceX equity, combined with the historical equity of X and xAI, adjusted for the respective exchange ratios used in the mergers.
*   **Stock Split Adjustment:** All share and per-share information has also been retroactively adjusted to reflect a five-for-one forward stock split effective May 4, 2026.

### **3. Notes on Historical Periods Presented as Combined**
Multiple sections explicitly state that historical data is presented as if the entities had always been combined.
*   The preamble notes that the "summary historical consolidated financial and operating data of SpaceX has been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the companies for all periods presented."
*   It further clarifies that the "consolidated financial statements have been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the net assets of the entities at their historical carrying amounts for all periods presented."
*   The notes confirm this constitutes a **"change in reporting entity."**

### **4. Specific Details on the Combination Mechanics**
*   **Merger Timeline:**
    *   X Holdings was acquired by xAI effective **March 28, 2025** ("X Merger").
    *   X.AI Holdings Corp. (xAI) was acquired by SpaceX effective **February 2, 2026** ("xAI Merger").
*   **Exchange Ratios (Applied Retroactively):**
    *   **X Merger:** Each share of X Common Stock was converted to 2.776 shares of xAI Common Stock.
    *   **xAI Merger:** Each share of xAI Common Stock was converted to 0.1433 shares of SpaceX Common Stock (on a pre-split basis).
*   **Retroactive Share Presentation:** As a result of these mergers, historical share counts for X, X.AI Corp., and xAI Common Stock are presented in the financial statements as if they had been converted into SpaceX Common Stock at the applicable exchange rates for all periods presented.
*   **Exception - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock:** xAI's Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock was legally outstanding as a separate class in all historical periods prior to the merger. Therefore, it is **not** recast on an as-converted basis. Its conversion into SpaceX Common Stock is recognized only in the period the exchange actually occurred (Q1 2026).

### **Summary Statement from the Prospectus:**
> "The consolidated financial statements of SpaceX have been retrospectively recast for all periods presented to include (i) the historical results of X.AI Holdings Corp... and X Holdings Corp.... because these transactions were between entities under common control, and (ii) a five-for-one stock split... This presentation constitutes a change in reporting entity."

**Sources:**
All information is derived from the sections labeled **[SECTION: PREAMBLE]** and **[SECTION: PREAMBLE]** within the provided document chunks.

steps.reasoning_content: Good, I now have the core disclosures. Let me also retrieve information about the segment-level financials, any disclaimers about comparability, and any risk factors or other disclosures that discuss the interpretability challenges.

arguments.prompt: Retrieve any disclosures about segment reporting, the breakdown of revenue and financial performance by segment (SpaceX launch, Starlink, xAI, X/Twitter), and any disclaimers about comparability of historical periods or limitations of the recast data from {{spacex_s1}}. Also retrieve any risk factors related to the combination of these businesses or integration risks.

results.content:
# Segment Reporting, Revenue Breakdown, Financial Performance, Comparability Disclaimers, and Integration Risk Factors

Based on the provided prospectus excerpts, here is a comprehensive retrieval of the requested disclosures:

---

## 1. Segment Reporting — Definitions and Structure

SpaceX operates **three reportable segments**:

> **Space segment** — designs, manufactures, and launches reusable rockets to provide high cadence, reliable, and affordable access to space at unprecedented scale.

> **Connectivity segment** — operates a worldwide high-speed, low-latency broadband network powered by thousands of Starlink satellites in Low-Earth Orbit, delivering connectivity to millions of consumer, enterprise, and government customers through the Starlink offering.

> **AI segment** — operates a vertically integrated AI platform spanning a frontier LLM (Grok), AI solutions for consumer and enterprise customers, X — a real-time information, entertainment, and free speech platform — and AI computational infrastructure.

---

## 2. Revenue Breakdown by Segment and Sub-Category

The prospectus provides the following revenue disaggregation categories:

| Segment | Sub-Category |
|---------|-------------|
| **Space** | Launch Services |
| | Launch & Development |
| **Connectivity** | Consumer |
| | Enterprise & Government *(includes Starlink Mobile)* |
| **AI** | Advertising |
| | AI Solutions & Infrastructure |

A footnote clarifies:
> *"Enterprise & Government revenue includes revenue from Starlink Mobile service offerings."*

### Revenue Recognition Policies by Segment

- **Launch Services**: Recognized at a **point in time** upon launch/deployment, due to interchangeability of flight hardware and minimal unique engineering costs.
- **Launch & Development**: Recognized **over time** using the cost-to-cost input method (contracts range from one to fourteen years).
- **Connectivity (broadband/mobile services)**: Recognized **over time** as the customer simultaneously receives and consumes benefits. Fixed-price services are recognized ratably over subscription term; variable-priced services based on actual data consumption.
- **Starlink Kits**: Point of sale.
- **Inter-segment Starlink launches**: No inter-segment revenue recognized; launch costs are capitalized in satellites in Property, plant, and equipment, net.

> *"We allocate a significant amount of launch capacity to our Connectivity segment, and expect to allocate a significant amount to our AI segment in the future. Our Space segment revenue only reflects our customer launches and customer activities."*

---

## 3. Financial Performance by Segment

### Space Segment

| Metric | Q1 2026 | Q1 2025 | FY 2025 | FY 2024 | FY 2023 |
|--------|---------|---------|---------|---------|---------|
| Revenue | $4,086M | — | — | — | — |
| Loss from operations | $(657)M | — | — | — | — |
| Segment Adjusted EBITDA | $653M | — | — | — | — |
| Capital expenditures (Q1 2026) | $1,052M | | | | |
| Capital expenditures (FY 2025) | $3,832M | | | | |
| R&D (Starship, Q1 2026) | $930M | | | | |
| R&D (Starship, FY 2025) | $3,004M | | | | |

> Revenue **decreased** $246M in Q1 2026 vs. Q1 2025 "due to lower Launch Services missions and timing of work for government [contracts]."
> Cost of revenue **decreased** $16M "due to less customer launches."
> R&D **increased** $404M "driven by accelerated investment in our Starship vehicle and related facilities."

### Connectivity Segment (Starlink)

| Metric | Q1 2026 | Q1 2025 | FY 2025 | FY 2024 | FY 2023 |
|--------|---------|---------|---------|---------|---------|
| Revenue | $3,257M | — | $11,387M | — | — |
| Income from operations | $1,188M | $1,033M | $4,423M | $2,006M | $469M |
| Segment Adjusted EBITDA | $2,087M | $1,618M | $7,168M | $3,849M | $1,602M |
| Starlink Subscribers | 10.3M | 5.0M | 8.9M | 4.4M | 2.3M |
| Starlink ARPU | $66/mo | $86/mo | $81/mo | $91/mo | $99/mo |
| Capital expenditures (Q1 2026) | $1,332M | | | | |
| Capital expenditures (FY 2025) | $4,178M | | | | |

**FY 2025 year-over-year growth:**
- Revenue: **+49.8%**
- Income from operations: **+120.4%**
- Segment Adjusted EBITDA: **+86.2%**

> Driven by "subscriber growth, increasing enterprise adoption, and continued improvement in network efficiency."

**Q1 2026 drivers:**
- Revenue increased $782M as "Starlink Subscriber base continued to grow."
- Cost of revenue increased $437M "driven by an increase in depreciation related to the number of satellites placed into orbit."

### AI Segment (including xAI and X/Twitter)

| Metric | Q1 2026 | Q1 2025 | FY 2025 | FY 2024 | FY 2023 |
|--------|---------|---------|---------|---------|---------|
| Revenue | $818M | — | $3,201M | — | — |
| Loss from operations | $(2,469)M | $(936)M | $(6,355)M | $(1,561)M | $(3,973)M |
| Segment Adjusted EBITDA | $(609)M | $(112)M | $(1,237)M | $347M | $1,222M |
| Nameplate compute draw (GW) | 1 | 0.3 | 0.8 | 0.3 | 0 |
| Capital expenditures (Q1 2026) | $7,723M | | | | |

**Q1 2026 drivers:**
- Revenue increased $91M "from higher X and Grok subscriptions."
- R&D increased $1,471M "driven by depreciation of GPU hardware, and the cost of cloud computing and data center infrastructure expenses as a result of our AI data center expansions."
- Cost of revenue increased $5M.

> *"In our newly acquired AI segment, we plan to prioritize growth and investment to capture significant opportunities in AI applications and compute infrastructure."*

---

## 4. Deferred Revenue

> *"Deferred revenue is recorded when cash payments are received or due, in advance of the Company's performance. Deferred revenue primarily relates to Space agreements and Connectivity enterprise and government contracts."*

- Total deferred revenue as of **December 31, 2024**: **$10,179 million**
- Of which recognized as revenue in FY 2025: **$4,080 million**
- Total deferred revenue as of **December 31, 2025**: **$12,116 million**

---

## 5. Disclaimers About Comparability and Limitations of Recast Data

### Retrospective Recasting for Common-Control Transactions

> *"The consolidated financial statements of SpaceX have been retrospectively recast for all periods presented to include (i) the historical results of X.AI Holdings Corp., which was acquired by SpaceX, effective February 2, 2026 (the 'xAI Merger'), and X Holdings Corp. ('X Holdings'), which was acquired by xAI, effective March 28, 2025 (the 'X Merger'), because these transactions were between entities under common control, and (ii) a five-for-one stock split of the Company's Class A, Class B, and Class C Common Stock, effective May 4, 2026 (the '2026 Stock Split')."*

> *"Unless otherwise stated or the context otherwise requires, all share and per share information included in this prospectus have been retroactively adjusted to reflect the 2026 Stock Split."*

### Historical Data Limitations

> *"The summary historical consolidated financial and operating data presented below is not indicative of the results to be expected for any future period, and the results for any interim period are not necessarily indicative of the results to be expected for the full fiscal period."*

> *"The summary historical consolidated financial and operating data of SpaceX has been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the companies for all periods presented to include the historical results of xAI, which was acquired by SpaceX, effective February 2, 2026, and X Holdings, which was acquired by xAI, effective March 28, 2025, because these transactions were between entities under common control."*

> *"The summary historical consolidated financial data included in this section is not intended to replace the consolidated financial statements and is qualified in its entirety by our consolidated financial statements and related notes included elsewhere in this prospectus."*

> *"The following information should be read together with 'Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations' and our consolidated financial statements and related notes thereto included elsewhere in this prospectus."*

### Forward-Looking Statement Caution

> *"This prospectus contains forward-looking statements that are subject to a number of risks and uncertainties, many of which are beyond our control."*

---

## 6. Risk Factors Related to Business Combination and Integration Risks

### AI Segment Integration Risk (Primary)

> *"The Company's AI segment is recently formed, still being integrated, operates in a rapidly evolving industry and is subject to integration, execution, competitive and operational risks."*

> *"We acquired xAI in February 2026 as the foundational platform for our AI segment and as part of our ambitious vertical integration strategy intended to combine artificial intelligence capabilities with our established Space and Connectivity businesses. Prior to its acquisition by the Company, xAI itself was an early-stage company. As a result, our AI segment remains in a relatively early stage of organizational and operational maturity and is subject to integration, scaling and execution risks."*

> *"The successful integration of acquired businesses, technologies, strategic partners, and employees is inherently complex, costly and time-consuming, and may result in operational inefficiencies, delays, disruptions, increased costs, loss of knowledge and diversion of management attention. As is common in large acquisitions, we have had to take significant steps to integrate xAI's operations into our broader corporate structure as part of our AI segment."*

### Strategic Transaction Integration Risks (Broader)

> *"We may not achieve the anticipated benefits, synergies, or strategic objectives of any acquisition, divestiture, or other strategic transaction in a timely manner, or at all, including those we expect from the recent acquisition of xAI, the acquisition of spectrum assets and licenses from EchoStar in connection with our Starlink Mobile initiatives, our collaboration on Terafab with Tesla, Intel or any future partners, project and our recent collaboration with Cursor and any potential acquisition of Cursor, if consummated."*

> *"Acquisitions, divestitures, or other strategic transactions may present unforeseen liabilities or disruptions to our operations, which could adversely impact our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects. We may assume unexpected obligations or incur costs associated with acquired businesses, including litigation, regulatory compliance, environmental liabilities, or contractual disputes, which could result in material losses or divert management focus from ongoing operations."*

### Nascent Industry and Multiplanetary Risk

> *"[Certain industries such as] manufacturing capabilities on the Moon and Mars, and asteroid mining, do not exist today. While we believe these industries will develop over time, the manner in which they emerge, including the timing of commercialization, the scale and pace of adoption, and the applicable competitive, technical, regulatory, geopolitical, and economic frameworks may differ materially from our current expectations."*

> *"Even if such goals are achieved, they may not generate meaningful revenue or achieve profitability for an extended period of time."*

---

**In summary**, the prospectus discloses three segments (Space/Connectivity/AI), provides detailed revenue disaggregation with sub-categories (including Starlink Mobile within Enterprise & Government), presents segment-level profitability metrics (income from operations and Segment Adjusted EBITDA) with historical trends, and includes multiple layers of comparability disclaimers—most critically that all historical periods have been retrospectively recast to include xAI (acquired Feb 2026) and X Holdings (acquired Mar 2025) as common-control transactions, making cross-period comparisons potentially misleading given the AI segment's early-stage, pre-integration nature.

steps.reasoning_content: Now let me also retrieve information about the specific Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation and non-GAAP measures to understand the full picture of how the recast affects key metrics.

arguments.prompt: Retrieve the Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation table and definition from {{spacex_s1}}, including the starting GAAP metric, each adjustment item, and any non-GAAP measure definitions. Also retrieve any disclosure about related-party transactions between SpaceX, xAI, and X that were eliminated in consolidation.

results.content:
Based on the provided text, here are the requested items:

### **1. Adjusted EBITDA and Segment Adjusted EBITDA: Definition & Reconciliation**

**Definitions (from the Preamble):**
*   **Adjusted EBITDA** is defined as net income (loss) excluding (i) depreciation and amortization, (ii) share-based compensation, (iii) impairment, (iv) restructuring charges, (v) interest expense, (vi) interest income, (vii) other income (expense), net, and (viii) provision for income taxes.
*   **Segment Adjusted EBITDA** is defined as segment income (loss) from operations excluding (i) depreciation and amortization, (ii) share-based compensation, (iii) restructuring charges, and (iv) impairment.

**Role as Performance Measures (from the Preamble):**
Adjusted EBITDA and Segment Adjusted EBITDA are key performance measures that management uses to assess financial performance, for internal planning, and for forecasting. They are considered meaningful performance measures for investors to evaluate operating performance and compare financial results.

**Reconciliation Table (Consolidated - Net Income to Adjusted EBITDA):**
| (in millions) | Three Months Ended March 31, 2026 | Three Months Ended March 31, 2025 | Year Ended December 31, 2025 | Year Ended December 31, 2024 | Year Ended December 31, 2023 |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Net income (loss)** | **$(4,276)** | **$(528)** | **$(4,937)** | **$791** | **$(4,628)** |
| **Add (deduct):** | | | | | |
| Depreciation and amortization | 2,442 | 1,443 | 6,701 | 3,824 | 2,635 |
| Share-based compensation | 639 | 232 | 1,947 | 784 | 679 |
| Restructuring charges | (11) | 4 | 487 | 213 | 237 |
| Impairments | — | 24 | 38 | 63 | 3,775 |
| Interest expense | 664 | 447 | 1,945 | 1,580 | 1,693 |
| Interest income | (213) | (117) | (492) | (371) | (249) |
| Other (income) expense, net | 1,876 | 211 | 177 | (985) | 42 |
| Provision for (benefit from) income taxes | 6 | 14 | 718 | (549) | (363) |
| **Adjusted EBITDA** | **$1,127** | **$1,730** | **$6,584** | **$5,350** | **$3,821** |

**Reconciliation Tables (Segment - Segment Income/Loss from Operations to Segment Adjusted EBITDA):**
*Tables for Q1 2026, Q1 2025, FY 2025, and FY 2024 are provided in the source text, showing adjustments for Depreciation & Amortization, Share-based Compensation, Restructuring Charges, and Impairments for the Space, Connectivity, and AI segments.*

---

### **2. Related-Party Transactions Eliminated in Consolidation**

The following transactions between SpaceX, xAI, X, and other entities affiliated with Elon Musk were identified as related-party transactions and have been eliminated in the consolidated financial statements.

**Transactions with Tesla (SpaceX & xAI):**
*   **SpaceX commercial agreements:** Obtained goods/services of $11M (2023), $4M (2024), $144M (2025), and $0.2M (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).
*   **xAI commercial, licensing, and support agreements:** Obtained goods/services of $191M (2024), $506M (2025), and $34M (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026). Recognized revenue of $2M (2025) and $0.4M (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).
*   **X Holdings advertising agreements:** Tesla purchased advertising of $0.5M (2024), $4M (2025), and $0 (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).
*   **Aircraft usage:** SpaceX charged Tesla $1M (2023), $1M (2024), $2M (2025), and $0 (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).

**Transactions with The Boring Company (X & SpaceX):**
*   **X Office Space Lease:** X made lease payments of $0.1M (2024), $1M (2025), and $0.1M (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).
*   **SpaceX Tunnel Construction:** SpaceX incurred $1M in expenses (2025) for tunnel construction in Bastrop, TX.

**Transactions with Musk Industries LLC (xAI):**
*   **xAI Real Property Lease:** xAI made lease payments of $0.5M (2024), $2M (2025), and $0.2M (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).

**Transactions with Musk-owned Security Company (SpaceX):**
*   **Security Services:** SpaceX incurred expenses of $2M (2023), $3M (2024), $4M (2025), and $1M (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).

**Transactions with Valor Equity Partners (xAI, affiliated with board member Antonio J. Gracias):**
*   **Equipment Lease Agreements:** Subsidiaries of xAI have three major equipment lease agreements with Valor for computing hardware, requiring aggregate cash payments of $6,986M, $6,633M, and $6,587M over the lease terms. SpaceX or its subsidiaries guarantee these obligations. Payments made were $885M (2025) and $857M (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).
*   **API Services:** X received payments from Valor of $1M (2024), $1M (2025), and $0.1M (Jan 1-Feb 28, 2026).

**Accounting Treatment (from Financial Notes):**
The mergers of xAI and X into SpaceX were accounted for as reorganizations under common control because Elon Musk had a controlling financial interest in all entities. Consequently, all historical related-party transactions between the entities **have been eliminated in consolidation** for all periods presented.

Let me also check for any specific disclosures about how the standalone SpaceX financial performance can or cannot be disentangled from the recast figures.

arguments.prompt: Retrieve any disclosures from {{spacex_s1}} about standalone SpaceX historical financial data, any carve-out or standalone financial information for SpaceX, xAI, or X prior to combination, and any explicit cautionary language about the inability to determine standalone historical performance. Also retrieve any discussion of the change in reporting entity accounting treatment and its implications.

results.content:
Based on the provided excerpts from the financial statement notes, here are the key disclosures you requested:

### 1. Disclosures on the Common-Control Mergers & Retrospective Effects
*   **Accounting Treatment & Retrospective Combination:** The mergers of xAI and X into SpaceX are accounted for as "reorganizations of entities under common control" because Elon Musk held a controlling financial interest in all entities. Consequently, the financial statements present the **retrospective combination** of the net assets of SpaceX, xAI, and X at their **historical carrying amounts for all periods presented**.
*   **Elimination of Transactions:** All historical related party transactions between SpaceX, xAI, and X have been eliminated in the consolidated statements.
*   **Equity Presentation:** The capital stock and shareholders' equity for all periods is presented as a **continuation of SpaceX's historical equity**, combined with the historical equity of X and xAI, adjusted by the merger exchange ratios. This constitutes a **change in reporting entity**.
*   **2026 Stock Split:** A five-for-one forward stock split was effected on May 4, 2026. **All share and per-share information has been retroactively adjusted** to reflect this split for all periods presented.

### 2. Standalone Historical Financial Data & Cautionary Language
*   **Provision of Standalone Data:** The notes explicitly state: **"As the consolidated financial statements already reflect the reorganization of entities under common control for all periods presented, separate financial statements of xAI and X are not provided."**
*   **Cautionary Language:** The prospectus includes clear warnings that:
    *   "The summary historical consolidated financial and operating data presented below is **not indicative of the results to be expected for any future period**, and the results for any interim period are not necessarily indicative of the results to be expected for the full fiscal period."
    *   The historical data should be read in conjunction with the MD&A and full financial statements.

### 3. Change in Reporting Entity: Accounting Treatment & Implications
*   **Nature of the Change:** The combination of these commonly controlled entities is treated as a **change in reporting entity**. The appropriate accounting treatment is to retroactively revise all prior periods as if the entities had always been combined.
*   **Implication for Comparability:** This retroactive revision means the financial statements for all prior periods **now include the results of operations, assets, and liabilities of xAI and X**. Therefore, **historical trends and financial figures are not directly comparable to the standalone, pre-merger financial statements of SpaceX**.
*   **Specific Treatment of xAI Preferred Stock:** An important exception is noted for xAI's Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock. Because it was a separate class of a distinct predecessor entity, its conversion into SpaceX common stock is recognized **only prospectively as of the xAI Merger date (February 2, 2026), and is not retrospectively applied**. It is presented as "Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock" in all periods prior to the merger.

In summary, the disclosures confirm that **no standalone historical carve-out financial statements for SpaceX, xAI, or X are available** due to the common-control accounting treatment. All historical data is presented on a combined, retrospective basis, with strong cautionary language that this combined history is not a guide for future results. The primary accounting implication is that the "change in reporting entity" requires the retrospective restatement of all periods to show the combined entity, fundamentally altering the basis of historical comparison.

arguments.final_result:
# Common-Control Recast Accounting: Investor-Interpretability and Transparency Concerns

## 1. Factual Basis of the Recast

The SpaceX S-1 (filed May 20, 2026) discloses that its consolidated financial statements have been **retrospectively recast under common-control accounting** to include:

- **X Holdings** (acquired by xAI effective March 28, 2025)
- **xAI** (acquired by SpaceX effective February 2, 2026)

The accounting basis is that Elon Musk held a controlling financial interest (majority voting interest) in all three entities throughout all periods presented. Under ASC 805-50, reorganizations of entities under common control require the surviving entity's financial statements to be prepared **as if the combining entities had always been combined**, with net assets carried at historical cost, no goodwill recognized, and all intercompany transactions eliminated.

Critically, the filing states: **"As the consolidated financial statements already reflect the reorganization of entities under common control for all periods presented, separate financial statements of xAI and X are not provided."**

---

## 2. Core Interpretability and Transparency Concerns

### A. Standalone SpaceX Performance Becomes Unobservable

The most fundamental concern is that **no standalone SpaceX financial statements are available for any period**. All historical figures—including the FY 2023, FY 2024, and FY 2025 data points—already incorporate the operations of xAI (an AI platform including Grok and compute infrastructure) and X Holdings (the social media/advertising platform formerly known as Twitter).

For an investor evaluating SpaceX's core launch services and Starlink businesses, the recast makes it impossible to:
- Isolate historical SpaceX-only revenue, cost structure, and profitability
- Assess the organic growth trajectory of the standalone SpaceX business pre-combination
- Benchmark SpaceX's operating margins or capital intensity against pure-play space and connectivity peers without the AI and social media segment contaminating the consolidated figures

### B. Artificial Historical Trend Continuity

The recast creates **historically continuous consolidated financial series that never existed in reality**. For example:

| Metric | FY 2023 (Recast) | FY 2024 (Recast) | FY 2025 (Recast) |
|--------|-----------------|-----------------|-----------------|
| Adjusted EBITDA | $3,821M | $5,350M | $6,584M |

These figures reflect the combined SpaceX + xAI + X entity for all three years, even though X Holdings only merged into xAI in March 2025, and xAI only merged into SpaceX in February 2026. An investor analyzing year-over-year "growth" in these figures cannot distinguish between:
- Genuine organic growth of SpaceX's launch and connectivity businesses
- The mechanical inclusion of X Holdings' and xAI's historical operations in earlier periods
- Changes in the operating performance of a social media platform and AI startup

This is particularly acute for the **AI segment**, which shows FY 2025 Segment Adjusted EBITDA of $(1,237)M versus FY 2024 of $347M—a swing that might reflect operational deterioration of X's advertising business, xAI's aggressive capital deployment, or a combination thereof, but cannot be decomposed without standalone data.

### C. Segment Reporting Cannot Fully Remedy the Problem

While SpaceX reports three segments (Space, Connectivity, and AI), the segment data itself is presented on a recast basis. The AI segment for FY 2023 includes the historical operations of X Holdings (when it was a separate, independent company under different management structures and capital allocation), which is fundamentally different from what the segment represents post-combination. The segment adjusted EBITDA trend:

| AI Segment | FY 2023 | FY 2024 | FY 2025 | Q1 2026 |
|-----------|---------|---------|---------|---------|
| Adj. EBITDA | $1,222M | $347M | $(1,237)M | $(609)M |

This shows a path from positive to deeply negative EBITDA, but the "FY 2023" figure reflects X Holdings' standalone results (predominantly a social media/advertising business), while "Q1 2026" reflects the combined AI segment with massive GPU infrastructure investment. The investor is comparing fundamentally different business configurations across a single segment time series.

### D. Eliminated Related-Party Transactions Obscure True Economic Flows

The filing discloses substantial related-party transactions with Tesla, The Boring Company, Musk Industries LLC, and Valor Equity Partners (an entity affiliated with board member Antonio J. Gracias). These have been **eliminated in consolidation** for all periods, including:

- xAI's $506M in commercial transactions with Tesla in 2025
- Valor's equipment lease agreements requiring aggregate payments of ~$20.2 billion
- SpaceX's $144M in Tesla transactions in 2025

While elimination is required under consolidation accounting, the result is that the historical consolidated statements do not reflect the actual cash flows and commercial relationships that existed between these entities when they were separately managed. An investor cannot reconstruct the true economic dependencies and counterparty relationships that characterized the pre-combination period.

### E. Capital Structure Distortion Across Periods

The exchange ratios applied retroactively—X shares converted at 2.776x xAI shares, xAI shares converted at 0.1433x SpaceX shares (pre-split), plus the retroactive 5:1 stock split—mean that historical share counts and per-share metrics are computed on a basis that never existed. The filing's exception for xAI's Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock (not retrospectively converted because it was legally a separate class) further complicates the picture, creating a hybrid capital structure in the historical periods that combines elements of prospective and retrospective treatment.

### F. Management Incentive and Narrative Framing

The common-control treatment enables management to present a longer financial history for what is effectively a newly combined entity, potentially:
- Deflecting scrutiny of the AI segment's massive losses ($6,355M operating loss in FY 2025) by embedding them in a longer historical series where they appear as a natural continuation
- Making the consolidated business appear larger and more diversified in historical periods than it actually was
- Enabling revenue and EBITDA growth narratives that may partly reflect the retroactive inclusion of X's advertising revenue or xAI's early operations

---

## 3. What the Filing Does and Does Not Disclose

**Explicitly disclosed:**
- All periods are recast; no standalone financials are provided
- The recast constitutes a "change in reporting entity"
- Intercompany transactions are eliminated
- Historical data "is not indicative of the results to be expected for any future period"

**Partially disclosed through segment data:**
- Three-segment breakdown allows some directional analysis, but segment data itself is recast

**Not disclosed:**
- Standalone SpaceX revenue, operating income, or EBITDA for any period
- Standalone xAI or X Holdings financial statements
- The specific impact of the recast on each line item in each historical period (i.e., no bridge from standalone to combined)
- Organic growth rates for any segment exclusive of the recast effect

---

## 4. Analytical Implications for Investors

The recast effectively means that **SpaceX's S-1 presents no historical period reflecting the company as investors are being asked to value it**. The combined entity—which spans reusable rockets, satellite broadband, a social media platform, a frontier LLM, and AI compute infrastructure—has no unrecast financial history. Investors must therefore:

1. **Rely heavily on forward-looking guidance** rather than historical trends, since the historical record is synthetic
2. **Exercise caution with year-over-year growth metrics**, which may be inflated or distorted by the recast inclusion of different business mixes in earlier periods
3. **Give greater weight to Q1 2026 figures** as the first period that reflects post-combination operations (though even Q1 2026 includes only two months of combined xAI-SpaceX operations, since the xAI merger closed February 2, 2026)
4. **Discount segment-level trend analysis** for the AI segment, which blends fundamentally different business configurations across its historical series
5. **Seek supplemental disclosures** during the IPO roadshow regarding standalone SpaceX performance, organic segment growth, and the specific contribution of xAI and X to consolidated metrics

---

## Summary

Common-control recast accounting, while GAAP-compliant, creates a fundamental **information asymmetry** in this filing: the issuer possesses standalone financial data for each entity but is not required to present it, and has affirmatively chosen not to do so. The resulting consolidated statements present a synthetic history that obscures the standalone performance of the core SpaceX business, blends fundamentally different business models into a single historical series, and limits investors' ability to independently assess organic growth, capital allocation efficiency, and the relative contribution of each business to consolidated results. This is analytically material given that the AI segment alone accounts for $7.7 billion in Q1 2026 capital expenditures and $6.4 billion in FY 2025 operating losses—figures whose historical trajectory cannot be independently verified without standalone data.

---

**Sources:**
- SpaceX S-1 filed May 20, 2026: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm (Preamble, Basis of Presentation, Summary Historical Financial Data, Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements—Common Control Mergers, Related Party Transactions, Segment Reporting, and Risk Factors)
